title: 10 best herbal teas for hangover
intro: Herbal teas can help with hangover symptoms due to their hydrating properties, which can help restore fluids and electrolytes lost through alcohol consumption. Some herbal teas, like ginger and chamomile, can also help soothe an upset stomach and promote relaxation, reducing nausea and anxiety. Additionally, certain herbal teas, such as milk thistle and dandelion, have been suggested to support liver function, which is essential for alcohol metabolism and detoxification. However, it's important to note that while herbal teas can help alleviate some hangover symptoms, they are not a cure-all and it's still crucial to drink responsibly and stay hydrated. Lastly, it's always best to consult with a healthcare professional before consuming herbal teas for medicinal purposes, especially if you have any pre-existing medical conditions or are taking any medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomile tea helps with hangover because it has calming properties that can alleviate symptoms such as anxiety and irritability, which are common after drinking alcohol. The tea's anti-inflammatory components can reduce the inflammation caused by alcohol, helping to ease headaches and muscle aches. Chamomile tea is also a natural diuretic, promoting the body to flush out toxins and rehydrate, thereby reducing the intensity of a hangover. Furthermore, its soothing effects on the digestive system can help settle an upset stomach and relieve nausea. Lastly, chamomile tea has been shown to improve sleep quality, allowing the body to rest and recover from the effects of alcohol.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Chamomile flowers are used for medicinal tea for hangover due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties which can help soothe an upset stomach and headaches.
Leaves: The leaves of chamomile contain flavonoids and essential oils that can help aid digestion and relieve symptoms of overindulgence.
Seeds: Chamomile seeds are rich in antioxidants and can help support liver function, which can be beneficial in reducing the symptoms of a hangover.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid that binds to benzodiazepine receptors in the brain, helping to reduce anxiety and promote sleep, which can be beneficial during a hangover.
Chamazulene: A chemical compound with anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce headaches and inflammation associated with hangovers.
Bisabolol: A terpene alcohol with anti-inflammatory and calming effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of hangover such as nausea and restlessness.
Matricin: A precursor to chamazulene, matricin has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that can help with hangover-related headaches and muscle pain.
Spiroether: A compound with antispasmodic properties that can help relieve abdominal cramps and improve digestion, which can be beneficial during a hangover.
remedy_recipe: Boil 8 ounces of water to 212°F (100°C) for 1-2 minutes to optimize chamomile compounds extraction.
Pour hot water over 1-2 teaspoons of dried chamomile flowers, submerging them fully in a teapot or teacup.
Steep the chamomile in hot water for 5-10 minutes for the flavors and beneficial compounds to infuse.
Strain the steeped chamomile flowers out of the tea, discarding the spent flowers.
Add honey or lemon to taste (optional), then sip and relax as chamomile works its soothing magic up to 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Peppermint tea helps with hangover because it is known to soothe the digestive system, which can be irritated after excessive alcohol consumption. The menthol present in peppermint tea can help in relaxing the gastrointestinal muscles, thereby alleviating symptoms such as nausea and abdominal pain. Peppermint tea is also a natural diuretic, promoting fluid balance and helping to restore essential electrolytes lost due to alcohol-induced dehydration. Furthermore, its calming properties can aid in reducing stress and anxiety often associated with hangovers, while the warm temperature of the tea can help increase blood circulation, expediting the body's metabolism and detoxification process. Lastly, peppermint tea is caffeine-free, offering a gentle alternative to coffee that won't exacerbate dehydration or cause further stomach irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Peppermint leaves are used in medicinal tea for hangovers due to their calming and soothing effects on the stomach.
Flowers: Peppermint flowers are rich in essential oils and anti-oxidants, making them helpful in alleviating hangover symptoms.
Seeds: Peppermint seeds contain menthol, which can help with digestion and reduce nausea, making them a good addition to medicinal tea for hangovers.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Acts as a smooth muscle relaxant, helping to alleviate tension headaches and upset stomachs commonly associated with hangovers.
Methyl salicylate: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation and pain in the body caused by alcohol consumption.
Limonene: Acts as a natural antioxidant, helping to repair liver damage caused by alcohol consumption.
Eugenol: Possesses analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce pain and inflammation in the body caused by alcohol consumption.
Flavonoids: Contribute to the antioxidant properties of peppermint, helping to protect cells from damage and reduce oxidative stress caused by alcohol.
Rosmarinic acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce inflammation and pain caused by alcohol consumption.
remedy_recipe: In a tea infuser, combine 1 teaspoon of crushed dried peppermint leaves with 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the peppermint mixture for 5-10 minutes to allow the flavors to meld and release their soothing properties.
While the tea is steeping, squeeze half a lemon into a mug for its detoxifying and hydrating benefits.
After steeping, pour the peppermint tea through a strainer into the mug with lemon juice.
Enjoy your hot Peppermint Tea for Hangover, allowing it to alleviate your symptoms and rehydrate your body.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginger tea helps with hangover because it contains natural ingredients that are effective in alleviating the symptoms associated with alcohol consumption. First, ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce the inflammation caused by alcohol, helping to soothe headaches and body aches. Second, ginger is known for its ability to aid digestion, making it easier for the body to process and eliminate toxins, which can help to reduce nausea and vomiting. Third, ginger tea can help to rehydrate the body, which is essential for recovering from a hangover as alcohol can cause dehydration. Fourth, ginger tea contains antioxidants that can help to protect the liver from damage caused by alcohol, promoting overall liver health. Lastly, ginger tea is a caffeine-free alternative to coffee, which can help to avoid the dehydrating effects of caffeine, making it a better choice for those looking to recover from a hangover.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Ginger rhizomes are commonly used to make medicinal tea for hangovers due to their potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as nausea and headaches.

Leaves: Ginger leaves have been used in traditional medicine to treat hangovers, as they contain high levels of antioxidants that can help to detoxify the liver and reduce the severity of hangover symptoms.
Flowers: Ginger flowers are less commonly used in medicinal tea for hangovers, but they do contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to soothe inflammation and reduce hangover symptoms.
Seeds: Ginger seeds are not typically used in medicinal tea for hangovers, but they contain compounds that can help to protect the stomach lining and alleviate nausea and vomiting.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols are the main medicinal constituents in ginger, known for their anti-inflammatory and anti-emetic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of nausea and inflammation associated with hangovers.
Shogaols: Shogaols, a pungent compound formed when ginger is dried or cooked, have been shown to help reduce inflammation and alleviate stomach discomfort, making them beneficial for hangover relief.

Paradols: Paradols, derived from gingerols, exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the liver from oxidative stress and reduce inflammation caused by alcohol consumption.

Ginger Diols: Ginger diols have been found to suppress gastric contractions and reduce gastric secretions, providing relief from symptoms of stomach irritation and nausea often experienced during a hangover.

Zingerone: Zingerone, a phenolic aldehyde found in ginger, has been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, aiding in the protection of liver cells and reduction of inflammation caused by alcohol consumption.
remedy_recipe: Peel and grate 1 inch of fresh ginger; steep in hot water for 10 minutes.
Squeeze juice of half a lemon into the ginger water; stir.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of honey (or to taste) and stir until dissolved.
Opionally, add a cinnamon stick and/or a few slices of fresh orange for added flavor.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with hangover because it contains antioxidants that support liver function and speed up the breakdown of alcohol and its toxic byproducts. Additionally, it has calming properties that can help alleviate anxiety and stress often associated with hangovers. Furthermore, lemon balm tea can restore the balance of essential neurotransmitters in the brain, such as GABA, which can be depleted due to excessive alcohol consumption. Its anti-inflammatory effects may also help reduce headaches and muscle cramps that are common hangover symptoms. Finally, lemon balm tea can rehydrate the body and replenish essential electrolytes lost due to alcohol-induced dehydration.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are used to make medicinal tea for hangover due to their high content of rosmarinic acid, which helps reduce inflammation and anxiety.
Flowers: Lemon balm flowers are rich in antioxidants and essential oils, making them an ideal addition to medicinal tea for hangover to support liver function and detoxification.
Stems: While not as potent as the leaves or flowers, lemon balm stems can still contribute to the tea's therapeutic effects, providing additional antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This compound has antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by alcohol consumption.
Citral: This lemon-scented compound has been shown to protect liver cells from damage and promote their recovery, which can help alleviate hangover symptoms.
Nerol: This compound has anti-inflammatory and relaxing effects, which may help reduce inflammation and anxiety caused by hangovers.
Terpinen-4-ol: This compound has been shown to aid in detoxification and has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce the severity of hangover symptoms.
β-caryophyllene: This compound has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects, which may help alleviate headaches and muscle pain associated with hangovers.
Oleanolic acid: This compound has been shown to protect liver cells and reduce oxidative stress, which may help reduce liver damage caused by alcohol consumption.
Ursolic acid: This compound has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help reduce the severity of hangover symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest - Pick 2 teaspoons of fresh lemon balm leaves or use 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Steep - Place leaves in a tea infuser and put in a cup, then pour boiling water over them, let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Sweeten (optional) - Stir in honey or sugar to taste, if desired.
Strain - Remove the tea infuser and discard the leaves.
Serve - Drink the tea while it's still warm, can be enjoyed several times a day as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Milk thistle tea helps with hangover because it promotes liver health and detoxification. The tea's active compound, silymarin, is an antioxidant that helps protect liver cells from damage caused by alcohol. It also aids in the removal of toxins from the body, including the byproducts of alcohol metabolism. Furthermore, milk thistle tea can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, both of which are exacerbated by alcohol consumption. Finally, it may also help restore glutathione levels, a key antioxidant that is often depleted after heavy drinking, thus alleviating hangover symptoms.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of the milk thistle plant are commonly used to make medicinal tea for hangovers due to their high concentration of silymarin, a flavonoid that helps protect the liver and speed up its recovery.

Leaves: The leaves of the milk thistle plant are sometimes used in teas for hangovers as they contain antioxidants that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by alcohol consumption.
Flowers: Milk thistle flowers are less commonly used in teas for hangovers, but they do contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help support liver function and promote detoxification.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: Silymarin is the most well-known medicinal constituent of milk thistle, and it is effective in reducing the severity of a hangover. It does this by helping to protect and repair liver cells, which can become damaged due to alcohol consumption.

Silybin: Silybin is a flavonoid lignan, and it is actually the most active component of silymarin. It has antioxidant properties that help to reduce oxidative stress in the liver, which can help to alleviate hangover symptoms.

Silychristin: Silychristin is another flavonoid lignan found in milk thistle, and it has been shown to have hepatoprotective effects. This means that it can help to protect the liver from damage, which can help to reduce the severity of a hangover.

Silydianin: Silydianin is the fourth and final flavonoid lignan found in milk thistle, and it has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. These properties can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can help to alleviate hangover symptoms.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of crushed milk thistle seeds with 1 cup of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the active compounds.
Strain the tea to remove the solid particles and seeds.
Drink the tea slowly, allowing it to soothe and detoxify your system.

remedies.remedy_desc: Dandelion tea helps with a hangover because it aids in liver function, which is essential for the body to break down and eliminate alcohol. The tea is a natural diuretic, helping the body to flush out toxins and rehydrate, which can alleviate symptoms such as headache and nausea. Dandelion tea is also rich in antioxidants, which can help to reduce inflammation and damage to cells caused by excessive alcohol consumption. Furthermore, it contains vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin C, potassium, and magnesium, which can help to replenish essential nutrients lost during a night of drinking. Finally, dandelion tea has been used in traditional medicine for its calming and soothing properties, which can provide relief from anxiety and restlessness associated with hangovers.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of dandelions are used in making medicinal tea for hangovers due to their ability to help improve digestion and liver function.
Leaves: Dandelion leaves are rich in nutrients and help to alkalize the blood and reduce inflammation, making them a good choice for hangover tea.
Flowers: The flowers of dandelions contain antioxidants that help to protect the liver from damage caused by alcohol consumption, and they can be used to make a soothing hangover tea.
Buds: The buds of dandelions are a great source of vitamins and minerals that can help to replenish the body after a night of drinking, making them a good addition to a hangover tea.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: This compound found in dandelion has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate headaches and general inflammation associated with hangovers.
Taraxacin: Taraxacin is a sesquiterpene lactone in dandelion that can stimulate bile production, aiding in digestion and the breakdown of alcohol metabolites.

Inulin: Dandelion contains inulin, a type of dietary fiber, which can help regulate blood sugar levels and promote the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, counteracting the negative effects of alcohol on the gut microbiome.
Potassium: Dandelion leaves are rich in potassium, an electrolyte lost through urination and sweating during alcohol consumption, helping to restore balance in the body.
Sesquiterpene glycosides: These compounds, such as dandelionin and taraxacin, can stimulate the liver's detoxification process and support alcohol metabolism, reducing the severity of hangovers.

Flavonoids: Dandelion contains flavonoids, like luteolin and quercetin, that demonstrate antioxidant and hepatoprotective properties, which can help protect the liver from alcohol-induced damage and reduce oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Collect: Pick 2-3 handfuls of fresh dandelion flowers from an organic, pesticide-free area; ensure petals are fully open and free of debris.
Rinse: Thoroughly rinse flowers under cold running water, gently agitating to remove dirt and insects, then pat dry.
Steep: Place flowers in a pot and add 4 cups of boiling water; let it steep for 15-20 minutes to extract the medicinal properties.
Strain: Strain the liquid using a fine mesh strainer or cheesecloth into a teapot or cup, discarding the spent flowers.
Serve: Drink the tea warm, up to three times a day, between meals, for a maximum of three days to alleviate hangover symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with a hangover because it contains compounds called glycyrrhizin and glabridin, which can aid in detoxifying the body and protecting the liver. These substances work by stimulating the production of enzymes that help break down alcohol and other toxins, thus speeding up the body's natural recovery process. Additionally, licorice tea can help soothe an upset stomach and alleviate symptoms such as nausea and vomiting, which are common during a hangover. Furthermore, licorice tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption, which can contribute to headaches and other hangover symptoms. Finally, licorice tea may also help replenish essential minerals and electrolytes lost during a night of heavy drinking, helping to restore balance to the body and alleviate dehydration, another common cause of hangover symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are used to make medicinal tea for hangovers due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, which helps to protect the liver and speed up alcohol metabolism.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the licorice plant are used in medicinal tea for hangovers because they contain liquiritin, a flavonoid that helps to protect liver cells and promote alcohol breakdown.

Leaves: The leaves of the licorice plant are used in medicinal tea for hangovers as they contain various compounds, including flavonoids and saponins, which help to soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation.
Flowers: The flowers of the licorice plant are used in medicinal tea for hangovers due to their antioxidant properties, which help to protect cells from damage caused by alcohol.

Seeds: The seeds of the licorice plant are used in medicinal tea for hangovers as they contain useful compounds like triterpenoids and coumarins, which help to stimulate the liver's detoxification processes.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This compound is the primary active component in licorice and has been shown to help protect the liver from damage, aiding in the detoxification process during a hangover.
Liquiritin: This flavonoid helps to moisturize and invigorate the skin, reducing the dryness and flushing often associated with hangovers.
Glabridin: An isoflavan, glabridin can help alleviate inflammation and redness, providing relief for headaches and general discomfort during a hangover.
Liquiritigenin: A flavonoid, liquiritigenin can help protect the stomach lining and reduce gastric irritation, providing relief from nausea and acid reflux.
isoliquiritigenin: As a flavonoid, isoliquiritigenin has been shown to have antioxidant properties, helping to eliminate free radicals and reduce oxidative stress during a hangover.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 tablespoon of dried licorice root and 1 cup of water, and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Pour the tea into a cup and let it cool to a drinkable temperature.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to hydrate and soothe your system.

remedies.remedy_desc: Nettle tea helps with hangover because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate symptoms such as headaches and nausea caused by the dehydration resulting from alcohol consumption. It also contains essential nutrients like vitamins A, C, and certain B vitamins, as well as minerals like iron, potassium, magnesium, and calcium, which can help replenish the body and speed up the detoxification process. Additionally, nettle tea is a natural diuretic, promoting fluid balance and helping the body flush out toxins more efficiently. Its soothing effects on the digestive system can also help with stomach upset and aid in regulating bowel movements. Furthermore, nettle tea's antioxidant properties can help combat oxidative stress and support liver function, which is essential in the metabolism and elimination of alcohol from the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Nettle leaves are often used in medicinal teas for hangovers due to their anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate headaches.
Stems: Nettle stems can be used in teas to help detoxify the body and support the liver's function in breaking down alcohol.
Flowers: Nettle flowers are rich in antioxidants that can help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by alcohol consumption.
Seeds: Nettle seeds contain essential amino acids and minerals that can help replenish nutrients lost during heavy drinking and reduce the severity of hangover symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Histamine: Helps with hangover by counteracting the effect of histamine-depleting alcohol, thereby restoring the balance of this neurotransmitter in the body.
Acetylcholine: Promotes the recovery from hangover by aiding in the restoration of this neurotransmitter, which is often depleted due to alcohol's effect on the body.
Serotonin: Helps improve mood and alleviate hangover symptoms by aiding in the restoration of this neurotransmitter, which is often depleted due to alcohol's effect on the body.
Quercetin: Acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by alcohol consumption.
Caffeic Acid: Helps protect the liver from damage caused by alcohol, and may help to speed up the detoxification process.
Chlorogenic Acid: Acts as an antioxidant, helping to reduce oxidative stress caused by alcohol consumption.
Rutin: Strengthens the capillaries, reducing the inflammation and discomfort associated with hangover.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 handfuls of fresh nettle leaves, rinse them thoroughly, and let them wilt for 10 minutes to reduce their bitterness.
Boil 4 cups of water, add the nettle leaves, reduce heat, and simmer for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, allowing the tea to cool for a few minutes.
Stir in honey or lemon to taste (optional), as it can help with the taste and soothe the stomach.
Drink the nettle tea warm, aiming to finish the whole batch over 1-2 hours to alleviate hangover symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Red clover tea helps with hangover because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help to reduce the symptoms of a hangover by combating the effects of alcohol on the liver and other organs. The tea's diuretic properties can also help to flush toxins from the body, while its calming effects may soothe a nervous or upset stomach. Additionally, red clover tea is known to contain compounds that can help to support the liver's detoxification processes, which can aid in the breakdown and elimination of alcohol from the body. Furthermore, red clover tea contains isoflavones, a type of phytoestrogen, which can help to regulate hormones and alleviate symptoms such as headaches and mood swings, common during hangovers.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in isoflavones, which can help ease inflammation and oxidative stress caused by alcohol consumption.
Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants, which can help protect liver cells from damage and support recovery.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids and amino acids, which can help replenish nutrients and support liver function.
Stems: Contain tannins that can help alleviate gastrointestinal discomfort and support healthy digestion.
remedy_constituents: Isopropanol: Red clover contains small amounts of isopropanol, a type of alcohol that can help to metabolize and clear the excess ethanol (the alcohol in drinks) more quickly, thus alleviating hangover symptoms.

Gallic Acid: Gallic acid in red clover has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the inflammation and oxidative stress caused by alcohol consumption, thereby helping to alleviate hangover symptoms.
Tannins: Tannins in red clover have astringent properties that can help to protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation, which can help to alleviate nausea and other hangover symptoms.
Saponins: Saponins in red clover can help to stimulate the liver and improve its function, which can help the body to metabolize and clear alcohol more quickly, thus reducing hangover symptoms.

Salicylic Acid: Salicylic acid in red clover has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce headaches and other hangover symptoms caused by inflammation and oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1-2 teaspoons of dried red clover flowers in a cup of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Sip the red clover tea slowly to help alleviate hangover symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Skullcap tea helps with a hangover because it functions as a natural sedative, easing anxiety and promoting relaxation, which can be beneficial in managing symptoms of a hangover. It contains antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by alcohol consumption, thereby alleviating hangover symptoms. Skullcap tea can also help to regulate glucose levels, which can become unbalanced due to alcohol intake, thus providing some relief from hangover-related fatigue and weakness. Furthermore, it has been suggested that skullcap tea may have hepatoprotective effects, potentially aiding in the prevention and treatment of alcohol-related liver damage, a common consequence of excessive alcohol consumption. Lastly, skullcap tea can help to rehydrate the body, as it contains essential vitamins and minerals, which can become depleted due to alcohol-induced diuresis, a contributing factor in the onset of hangover symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the skullcap plant are rich in flavonoids and tannins, which can help soothe and relax the body, reducing symptoms of hangover.
Flowers: The flowers of the skullcap plant contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help protect the liver and reduce inflammation caused by alcohol consumption.
Stems: The stems of the skullcap plant are a good source of minerals and vitamins, which can help replenish the body and support its natural detoxification processes, alleviating hangover symptoms.

Seeds: The seeds of the skullcap plant contain essential oils and fatty acids, which can help lubricate the digestive system and promote the absorption of nutrients, reducing the severity of hangover.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by alcohol consumption.
Flavoneglycosides: Protect liver cells from damage and support liver function.
Phenolic acids: Help break down alcohol and eliminate it from the body.
Tannins: Astringent properties that can help alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting.
Sesquiterpenes: May help reduce anxiety and agitation associated with hangover.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, add 1 tablespoon of dried skullcap herb to 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the medicinal properties.
Strain the liquid to remove the herbs and ensure a smooth tea.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, for improved flavor.
Sip the skullcap tea slowly, allowing it to alleviate hangover symptoms gradually.

remedies.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with hangover because it is known to stimulate digestion and promote the faster removal of toxins, such as alcohol, from the body. The herb contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the liver and other organs, which are often affected by alcohol consumption. Yarrow tea is also known to soothe stomach irritation and settle nausea, common symptoms of a hangover. Additionally, it has been suggested that yarrow may have properties that help balance blood sugar levels, which can be disrupted by excessive alcohol consumption, further contributing to hangover symptoms. Lastly, yarrow tea can help promote relaxation and improve sleep quality, which can aid in recovery from the disrupted sleep patterns often associated with heavy drinking.

Burdock tea helps with a hangover because it promotes liver function, which is essential in breaking down and eliminating toxins, such as alcohol, from the body. The tea is a natural diuretic, helping the body to flush out toxins more efficiently, thereby reducing the severity of hangover symptoms. Burdock tea is also known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the headaches and body aches often associated with hangovers. Furthermore, the tea contains antioxidants that help protect liver cells from damage caused by excessive alcohol consumption, and it can also help rehydrate the body, counteracting the dehydrating effects of alcohol that contribute to hangover symptoms.

Catnip tea helps with a hangover because it contains nepetalactone, a compound that aids in reducing inflammation and soothing headaches caused by alcohol consumption. Additionally, catnip is known for its calming properties, which can alleviate anxiety and restlessness that often accompany hangovers. Moreover, it promotes digestion, helping to settle an upset stomach and reduce nausea. Catnip tea also rehydrates the body, as alcohol is a diuretic and can lead to dehydration, a significant contributor to hangover symptoms. Lastly, the soothing warmth of the tea can provide comfort and relaxation, helping to ease the discomfort and irritability often experienced during a hangover.

Hawthorn tea helps with hangover because it contains antioxidants that assist in detoxifying the liver, which is crucial in the processing and elimination of alcohol from the body. Additionally, hawthorn has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the body, including the brain, thereby alleviating hangover symptoms such as headaches and nausea. Furthermore, hawthorn tea can help regulate blood sugar levels, which tend to fluctuate after heavy drinking, contributing to hangover symptoms. The tea also contains tannins that can help tighten and soothe the lining of the stomach, reducing stomach irritation and discomfort. Lastly, hawthorn's calming effects on the nervous system may help manage anxiety and restlessness often associated with hangovers, promoting relaxation and restorative sleep.

Basil tea helps with hangover because it contains properties that can help the body recover from the effects of alcohol. For instance, basil is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the inflammation caused by alcohol in the body. Additionally, basil is rich in antioxidants that can help the body eliminate toxins, including those produced by alcohol metabolism. Basil tea can also help rehydrate the body, as alcohol is a diuretic and can lead to dehydration, one of the main causes of hangover symptoms. Furthermore, basil tea has been shown to help settle the stomach and improve digestion, which can be helpful in alleviating nausea and vomiting associated with hangovers. Finally, basil tea can help alleviate stress and promote relaxation, which can be beneficial in reducing the anxiety and irritability that often accompany hangovers.

Calendula tea helps with hangover because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate symptoms such as headaches and stomach aches caused by alcohol consumption. Its soothing effect on the digestive system aids in the faster metabolism and elimination of toxins, thereby reducing the duration of a hangover. Furthermore, calendula tea is known for its antioxidant properties that help repair cell damage caused by excessive alcohol intake. Additionally, it can help rehydrate the body, as alcohol is a diuretic and can lead to dehydration, which is a common cause of hangover symptoms. Lastly, the calming effect of calendula tea can also help reduce anxiety and promote better sleep, which are often affected during a hangover.

Fennel tea helps with hangover because it aids in rehydrating the body and replenishing essential minerals and electrolytes lost due to alcohol consumption. The herbal tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help reduce inflammation in the body, particularly in the liver, where alcohol is processed. Moreover, fennel tea promotes the production of digestive enzymes, facilitating the faster metabolism and elimination of alcohol and its toxic byproducts from the system. Additionally, as a natural diuretic, fennel tea can assist in flushing out toxins, further alleviating hangover symptoms. Lastly, fennel tea's calming effects on the stomach and digestive tract may help soothe feelings of nausea and discomfort often associated with hangovers.

Sage tea helps with hangover because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that work to reduce the inflammation and oxidative stress caused by alcohol consumption. Additionally, sage has been shown to help settle the stomach and aid in digestion, which can help alleviate symptoms such as nausea and abdominal pain. The calming properties of sage may also help to reduce anxiety and promote restorative sleep, further assisting in the recovery process. Moreover, sage has been used traditionally as a diuretic, helping to eliminate toxins from the body and relieve symptoms such as headaches and dehydration. Lastly, the soothing and comforting effects of drinking warm sage tea can provide a psychological boost, making it a potentially effective natural remedy for managing hangover symptoms.

Rosemary tea helps with hangover because it contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that aid in the detoxification process, speeding up alcohol metabolism and reducing the severity of symptoms. The rosmarinic acid present in rosemary is known to protect the liver, an organ significantly affected during heavy alcohol consumption. Furthermore, it helps alleviate nausea, a common hangover symptom, by promoting digestion and soothing the gastrointestinal tract. Rosemary tea's ability to relax muscles and improve circulation enhances the body's capacity to recover from the diuretic effects of alcohol, rehydrating the body and easing headaches. Lastly, the calming properties of rosemary tea can help reduce stress and anxiety associated with hangovers, providing overall mental well-being and promoting a quicker recovery.

Thyme tea helps with hangover because it contains antioxidants that speed up the oxidation of alcohol, thereby reducing its toxic effects on the body. Additionally, thyme is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms such as headaches and muscle aches associated with hangovers. Furthermore, thyme tea can help rehydrate the body, as alcohol is a diuretic that can lead to dehydration, one of the main causes of hangover symptoms. The herb also contains potassium and other electrolytes that can help restore the body's balance after a night of drinking. Finally, thyme tea can help settle the stomach and improve digestion, reducing nausea and promoting a faster recovery from the aftermath of excessive alcohol consumption.
